description: Phishing templates impersonating utility companies using disconnection fear and credit lures

templates.name: Power Outage Credit Notice
suggested_subject_lines: Power outage credit available on your account — claim now
Your utility credit is waiting — verify your account
Account credit notification: $45.20 applied to your balance
templates.notes: Credit/refund lure is highly effective — people don't want to miss free money. Works in areas with known utility outages.

templates.name: Utility – Autopay Payment Failed
suggested_subject_lines: Autopay failed - update your billing information
Action required: your automatic payment was declined
We couldn't process your utility payment
templates.notes: Autopay-failure billing lure. Universally relatable; good beginner-level test for the general population.

templates.name: Utility – Service Disconnection Notice
suggested_subject_lines: Action required to avoid service interruption
Your service is scheduled for disconnection
templates.notes: Disconnection-threat lure raises urgency over the autopay template. Use sparingly; high-pressure scenario.
